title: carmichaelia compacta
intro: Carmichaelia compacta, also known as the New Zealand broom or Carmichaelia, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand. It is a shrub-like plant with small, yellowish flowers and has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been reported to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
